audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for renewed blessing, food/blood boundary, image-of-God homicide law, covenant, rainbow sign, Noah vineyard episode, and Canaan curse. Final source-critical review may refine difficult Ham/Canaan interpretive issues.

verses.restored: And the Divine blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth.

familiar.text: And God blessed Noah and his sons, and said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.

notes.title: Renewed creation blessing
notes.body: The blessing echoes Genesis 1:28, restarting the human vocation after the flood.

verses.restored: The fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every living being of the earth, upon every winged creature of the heavens, upon everything that creeps on the ground, and upon all the fish of the sea. Into your hand they are given.

familiar.text: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth... into your hand are they delivered.

notes.title: Fear and dread
notes.body: The post-flood human-animal relationship differs from Genesis 1. Fear now marks the relationship.

verses.restored: Every moving thing that lives shall be food for you. As I gave you the green plant, I now give you everything.

familiar.text: Every moving thing that liveth shall be meat for you; even as the green herb have I given you all things.

notes.title: Food expansion
notes.body: Animal food is permitted here after the flood, expanding beyond the plant-food vision of Genesis 1.

verses.restored: Only flesh with its life, its blood, you shall not eat.

familiar.text: But flesh with the life thereof, which is the blood thereof, shall ye not eat.

notes.title: Blood and life
notes.body: Nephesh is linked with blood. Even when animal food is permitted, life-blood is restricted.

verses.restored: And surely your blood, for your lives, I will require. From every living being I will require it, and from the hand of the human, from the hand of each one’s brother, I will require the life of the human.

familiar.text: And surely your blood of your lives will I require; at the hand of every beast will I require it...

notes.title: Require blood
notes.body: The text establishes accountability for human bloodshed from animal and human alike.

verses.restored: Whoever sheds the blood of the human, by the human shall his blood be shed, for in the image of the Divine he made the human.

familiar.text: Whoso sheddeth man's blood, by man shall his blood be shed: for in the image of God made he man.

notes.title: Image and bloodshed
notes.body: Human life is protected because the human bears the Divine image. The verse grounds homicide accountability in image theology.

verses.restored: And you, be fruitful and multiply. Swarm on the earth and multiply in it.

familiar.text: And you, be ye fruitful, and multiply; bring forth abundantly in the earth, and multiply therein.

verses.restored: And the Divine said to Noah and to his sons with him, saying,

familiar.text: And God spake unto Noah, and to his sons with him, saying,

verses.restored: And I, look, I am establishing my covenant with you and with your seed after you,

familiar.text: And I, behold, I establish my covenant with you, and with your seed after you;

notes.title: Covenant with seed
notes.body: The covenant extends beyond Noah to future generations.

verses.restored: and with every living being that is with you: the winged creatures, the animals, and every living being of the earth with you, from all that came out of the ark to every living being of the earth.

familiar.text: And with every living creature that is with you, of the fowl, of the cattle...

notes.title: Covenant includes animals
notes.body: The covenant is not only with humans. It includes all living beings that came from the ark.

verses.restored: I establish my covenant with you: never again shall all flesh be cut off by the waters of the flood, and never again shall there be a flood to ruin the earth.

familiar.text: And I will establish my covenant with you; neither shall all flesh be cut off any more by the waters of a flood...

notes.title: Never again
notes.body: The covenant restrains future total flood-destruction.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, This is the sign of the covenant that I am giving between me and you and every living being that is with you, for generations of ages.

familiar.text: And God said, This is the token of the covenant which I make between me and you... for perpetual generations:

verses.restored: I set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.

familiar.text: I do set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a covenant between me and the earth.

notes.title: Bow
notes.body: Qeshet can mean bow, including a war bow. Set in the cloud, it becomes the sign of peace/restraint.

verses.restored: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow is seen in the cloud,

familiar.text: And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud:

verses.restored: I will remember my covenant that is between me and you and every living being of all flesh. The waters shall never again become a flood to ruin all flesh.

familiar.text: And I will remember my covenant... and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.

notes.title: Divine remembering
notes.body: The bow is a sign of covenantal remembering and restraint.

verses.restored: The bow shall be in the cloud, and I will see it, to remember the everlasting covenant between the Divine and every living being of all flesh that is on the earth.

familiar.text: And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant...

verses.restored: And the Divine said to Noah, This is the sign of the covenant that I have established between me and all flesh that is on the earth.

familiar.text: And God said unto Noah, This is the token of the covenant, which I have established between me and all flesh...

verses.restored: The sons of Noah who came out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Ham was the father of Canaan.

familiar.text: And the sons of Noah, that went forth of the ark, were Shem, and Ham, and Japheth: and Ham is the father of Canaan.

notes.title: Canaan introduced
notes.body: Canaan is named before the vineyard episode because the later curse falls on Canaan.

verses.restored: These three were the sons of Noah, and from these the whole earth spread out.

familiar.text: These are the three sons of Noah: and of them was the whole earth overspread.

verses.restored: Noah began to be a man of the ground, and he planted a vineyard.

familiar.text: And Noah began to be an husbandman, and he planted a vineyard:

notes.title: Man of the ground
notes.body: Noah is linked again with adamah, ground. The post-flood story returns to earth, agriculture, and vulnerability.

verses.restored: He drank from the wine and became drunk, and he uncovered himself inside his tent.

familiar.text: And he drank of the wine, and was drunken; and he was uncovered within his tent.

notes.title: Uncovered
notes.body: The text is terse. Later interpretation debates what happened, but the source says Noah became drunk and uncovered himself.

verses.restored: Ham, father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father and told his two brothers outside.

familiar.text: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brethren without.

notes.title: Saw nakedness
notes.body: The phrase may be simple seeing or may carry sexual/violation overtones in biblical idiom. The audit preserves the wording and flags ambiguity.

verses.restored: Shem and Japheth took the garment, placed it on both their shoulders, walked backward, and covered the nakedness of their father. Their faces were turned backward, and they did not see their father’s nakedness.

familiar.text: And Shem and Japheth took a garment... and covered the nakedness of their father...

notes.title: Covering without seeing
notes.body: Shem and Japheth act to cover vulnerability without gazing upon it.

verses.restored: Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his youngest son had done to him.

familiar.text: And Noah awoke from his wine, and knew what his younger son had done unto him.

notes.title: Done to him
notes.body: The wording suggests more than passive seeing may be involved, but the exact act remains debated.

verses.restored: And he said, Cursed is Canaan. A servant of servants shall he be to his brothers.

familiar.text: And he said, Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be unto his brethren.

notes.title: Canaan, not Ham
notes.body: The curse is pronounced on Canaan, not on Ham as a whole and not on all descendants later racialized as Hamites. This text has been badly misused and must be handled carefully.

verses.restored: And he said, Blessed be the Eternal, the Divine of Shem; and let Canaan be servant to him.

familiar.text: And he said, Blessed be the LORD God of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.

terms.display: the Eternal, the Divine of Shem

verses.restored: May the Divine enlarge Japheth, and may he dwell in the tents of Shem; and let Canaan be servant to him.

familiar.text: God shall enlarge Japheth, and he shall dwell in the tents of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.

notes.title: Japheth wordplay
notes.body: The verb enlarge sounds like Japheth, creating wordplay.

verses.restored: Noah lived after the flood three hundred fifty years.

familiar.text: And Noah lived after the flood three hundred and fifty years.

verses.restored: All the days of Noah were nine hundred fifty years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Noah were nine hundred and fifty years: and he died.
